mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes, with out-of-sample Rank IC consistently below 0.02 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration and falling consumer income, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.5 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances when UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy dropping below 52% during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month specifically during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases produce negative or flat 20-day forward returns during expansion periods, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below zero over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product levels indicate robust aggregate economic activity, which systematically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week via improved corporate revenue forecasts.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns, with a t-statistic for the slope coefficient below 1.5 in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates above 3 percent annually while policy rates stay near zero, markets price in imminent aggressive monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% year-over-year growth while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3 percent and FEDFUNDS stays below 1 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as wage growth supports consumption.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 20-day forward returns in over 55% of observed cases during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales during periods of elevated inflation signal eroding real consumer purchasing power, which immediately suppresses S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines during high inflation periods fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy dropping below 45%.
